Code fragment: public final TableField<VacationDestinationRecord, Long> DESTINATION_ID = createField(DSL.name("destination_id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-generated primary key column (DESTINATION_ID) even though a natural composite key using (DESTINATION_ID, COUNTRY_NAME) exists. Consider using just COUNTRY_NAME combined with a reference to the vacation as the primary key, or evaluate if the synthetic key is truly necessary.

Code fragment: public final TableField<CustomerRecord, LocalDateTime> LAST_UPDATE = createField(DSL.name("last_update"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME(6).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("now()"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME)), this, "");
Explanation: The LAST_UPDATE column has a default value but is marked as NULLABLE, which is inconsistent since a column with a default value that auto-populates should never be NULL in practice. Consider adding .nullable(false) to the column definition to enforce this constraint.

Code fragment: public final TableField<FilmRecord, MpaaRating> RATING = createField(DSL.name("rating"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR.defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("'G'::mpaa_rating"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR)).asEnumDataType(MpaaRating.class), this, "");
Explanation: The 'rating' column uses an ENUM type (MpaaRating) to restrict allowed values, which makes it difficult to add, remove, or modify rating options without altering the database schema. Consider creating a separate 'mpaa_rating' lookup table with a foreign key reference to allow easier maintenance of rating values.
